entries.title: FDIC Plans to Block Stablecoins From Pass-Through Insurance
entries.snippet: The Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) plans to propose rules saying that stablecoins are not eligible for FDIC pass-through insurance, and that tokenized deposits are eligible for such insurance. FDIC Chairman Travis Hill said this on Wednesday (March 11) in a speech delivered at the Amer
entries.tweet_text:
Will the FDIC's push to recognize tokenized deposits while blocking stablecoin insurance reshape digital asset regulation?

entries.title: Can Crypto's Open Network Dreams Survive Going Corporate?
entries.snippet: The reality of financial blockchain adoption is turning out to be a very different story than the permissionless approach the digital asset space was built atop philosophically. Institutions including JPMorgan Chase, Goldman Sachs, Citi, HSBC and BNY Mellon have all launched blockchain-based platfor
entries.tweet_text:
Crypto's founding promise of permissionless finance is colliding with reality as @JPMorgan, @GoldmanSachs, and other giants build proprietary blockchains.

The question: Can decentralization survive institutionalization?

entries.snippet: Squarefi emerged from stealth Tuesday (March 10) and announced its stablecoin-based financial infrastructure for global business payments. Stablecoin rails are quietly replacing traditional correspondent banking—watch for enterprise adoption to accelerate.

entries.title: The FCA Is Building a Sterling Stablecoin Market. The Rules That Kick In When It Succeeds Could Kill It.
entries.snippet: The FCA Is Building a Sterling Stablecoin Market. The Rules That Kick In When It Succeeds Could Kill It. FinTech Weekly
entries.tweet_text:
The FCA is laying groundwork for sterling stablecoins—but the regulatory framework designed to protect consumers once adoption succeeds could strangle the market before it matures.

Watch how compliance costs reshape the competitive landscape.
